scenario: Beacon Bank, a regional bank processing 6500 requests per hour, signs Camunda 8.8 client assertions with a PKCS12 keystore. The team sets camunda.client.auth.client-assertion.keystore-password but leaves camunda.client.auth.client-assertion.keystore-key-password unset, and must confirm the documented fallback the Spring Boot starter applies.
question: Which behavior does the 8.8 properties reference document when keystore-key-password is left unset under camunda.client.auth.client-assertion?

options.text: The reference documents that the keystore password value is reused as the key password when keystore-key-password is left unset on the client-assertion entry.
The reference documents that the OAuth client-secret value is reused as the key password when keystore-key-password is left unset on the client-assertion entry.
The reference documents that the Spring Boot starter fails fast when keystore-key-password is left unset on the client-assertion entry and there is no fallback.
The reference documents that a blank password is sent to the keystore when keystore-key-password is left unset on the client-assertion entry by default.

a.text: Correct. The 8.8 reference says keystore-key-password is the password of the key referenced by the alias and that if not set, the keystore password is used.

b.text: Incorrect. The client-secret is not a fallback for the keystore key password; the documented fallback is the keystore password under the same client-assertion section.

c.text: Incorrect. The starter does not fail; the reference documents a graceful fallback to the keystore password under camunda.client.auth.client-assertion.

d.text: Incorrect. The starter does not silently send a blank password; the documented behavior is to reuse the keystore password for the key.

explanation: Under camunda.client.auth.client-assertion, if keystore-key-password is not set, the Camunda 8.8 starter reuses the keystore-password to unlock the signing key referenced by the alias.

docs.title: Camunda Spring Boot starter properties reference
